target.title: feat: add coder/mux built-in adapter
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `mux` ([coder/mux](https://github.com/coder/mux)) as a built-in ACP agent. `acpx mux` resolves to `npx -y mux acp`, which launches coder/mux's ACP stdio bridge — no global install required.

mux exposes a real ACP agent via `mux acp` (built on `@agentclientprotocol/sdk`, stdio JSON-RPC) and ships as the unscoped npm package `mux`, so it slots into the existing npx-backed adapter pattern — identical in shape to `kilocode`/`opencode` — as a one-line registry entry with no core changes (it resolves through the generic spawn path, and `mux acp` auto-starts an in-process server).

## Changes

- `src/agent-registry.ts` — add `mux: "npx -y mux acp"` (alphabetical, between `kiro` and `opencode`)
- `test/agent-registry.test.ts` — registry resolution + ordering test
- `agents/Mux.md` — agent doc (mirrors the `fast-agent` template)
- `docs/agents.md` — registry table row + per-agent section
- `README.md`, `docs/install.md`, `agents/README.md`, `skills/acpx/SKILL.md` — registry/doc entries
- `CHANGELOG.md` — Unreleased entry

## Notes

- **Unpinned** (`npx -y mux acp`), matching `kilocode`/`opencode`; mux is pre-1.0 and fast-moving.
- mux needs a model-provider key (e.g. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`) configured before prompting — documented in `agents/Mux.md`.
- **SDK skew:** mux bundles `@agentclientprotocol/sdk ^0.14.1` vs acpx's `^0.22.1`; both speak ACP protocol v1 via `initialize` negotiation. The core loop should interoperate; a live handshake smoke-test is still pending a provider key.

## Testing

- `node --test dist-test/test/agent-registry.test.js` → 16/16 pass (incl. the new `mux` test and the `listBuiltInAgents` ordering assertion)
- markdownlint, `oxfmt --check`, and `oxlint --type-aware` clean on changed files; pre-commit build (tsdown) passes
